chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 180 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.877/2025. 2. For hearing of main case. 23.01.2025 Mr. S. Aamir Ali, advocate for the petitioner. On the last date, following order was passed: “16.01.2025 Syed Amir Ali, Advocate for Petitioner. 1. Granted. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3-4. Learned counsel for the petitioner is confronted as to maintainability hereof as to why the confiscation, destruction or deportation or export order issued under the Pakistan Plant Quarantine Act, 1976 may be interfered directly in writ jurisdiction. Learned counsel is confronted with question as to whether any remedial forum is provided by law and further whether the matter can even otherwise be entertained pursuant to judgment illumined by the Supreme Court in the case of Gul Taiz Khan Marwat vs. The Registrar, Peshawar High Court, Peshawar (PLD 2021 SC 391). To come up on 23.01.2025.” Today, maintainability could not be satisfied on the aforesaid. Learned counsel was queried as to whether the cause is actuated by any specific import and he replied in affirmative. Counsel was queried as to whether the requisite GD was assessed and if aggrieved, the petitioner had taken recourse in accordance with law. Counsel is unable to assist in such regard. In view of foregoing, petition is found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
